surfaced_from: PWA chat 2026-05-24 — John authorized temporary direct OpenClaw self-editing for repair speed and explicitly requested a backlog item to lock it down later
capability_needed: Governed OpenClaw self-repair/edit mechanism to replace the temporary direct-edit exception
details: While OpenClaw is being fixed, John authorized OpenClaw to directly edit its own files for speed. This is allowed as a temporary repair/debugging exception, not a normal operating mode. The future capability should lock this down with a safer governed mechanism: scoped write permissions, approvals or policy gates, backups/diffs, audit trail, and verification before restart or promotion.
search_trail: Asked OpenClaw on 2026-05-24 which document should record the temporary policy and what guardrails should apply; OpenClaw answered AGENTS.md plus BACKLOG.md/logs/backlog, with backup/diff/verification guardrails.
Verified OpenClaw global install with command -v/npm root/test -d: /usr/bin/openclaw points to global install under /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw.
Read /opt/cto/AGENTS.md and /opt/cto/BACKLOG.md; backlog schema supports a fork-trigger entry for source/governance changes outside normal Hermes Phase 1-4 scope.
proposed_resolution: Design and implement a governed OpenClaw self-repair mode or policy layer that allows direct source/config edits only under explicit scope, records every edit, creates rollback artifacts, and can be disabled once the repair phase is over.
blast_radius: Changes OpenClaw repair workflow and possibly file-system/tool permissions for /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw and related state under ~/.openclaw. It should reduce accidental self-modification risk without blocking urgent repair work.
rollback_plan: Disable the governed self-repair mode or restore previous permissions/policy, revert any OpenClaw source changes from backup or package reinstall, and keep the temporary AGENTS.md exception only until a better replacement is proven.
